iteration_1.changes: Rewrote skill from verbose reference to concise recipe-driven format with two focused recipes: log parsing → JSON and code cleanup with search-and-replace. Added explicit code examples and critical rules.

iteration_2.changes: Made var→let/const replacement instructions more emphatic with explicit examples for each variable type and bold emphasis on changing every var.

iteration_3.changes: Changed var replacement guidance to follow prompt-specified const variables exactly — only variables explicitly named in the prompt get const, all others get let. Added instruction to go through each function carefully.

key_insights: Original verbose reference skill was too large for devstral-small — model ignored most content
Recipe-driven format with concrete code examples works much better for small models
Model preferred manual file editing over running scripts — skill must guide the manual approach
var→let/const replacement needed very explicit rules about which variables get const (only prompt-named ones)
The model tends to skip transformations if not strongly emphasized — bold emphasis and repetition helps
